mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer spending strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both positive MoM
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive 60d returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapid capacity utilization and earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during recovery periods over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 2.5 percent trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above 2.5% are followed by positive 20d returns in > 55% of cases, invalidating the tightening fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll employment growth expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth confirms robust domestic demand, lifting equity prices and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both positive YoY
failure_condition: Combined GDP and retail sales growth fails to produce positive 20d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals demand destruction and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines MoM while FEDFUNDS rises
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling production and rising rates yields positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances.
